sanskrit:
अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते।
तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः॥
transliteration:
anye tu evam ajānantaḥ śrutvānyebhya upāsate |
te 'pi cātitaranty eva mṛtyuṁ śruti-parāyaṇāḥ ||
literal_meaning: Others, however, not knowing thus, having heard from others, begin to worship; they too certainly cross over death, being devoted to hearing.
contextual_meaning: Even without direct knowledge, sincere listening to trustworthy voices can launch a transformative process that dissolves existential dread.
psychological_state: Humble receptivity replaces the need for prior expertise; vicarious learning becomes a legitimate path to transcend death-anxiety.
trigger_scenario: A skeptical client with no spiritual background begins nightly YouTube guided meditations after overhearing a colleague’s story of recovery; within weeks their panic attacks diminish.
anchor_text: Listen deeply to wise voices and faith will carry you beyond death.
